module.name: terminal
module.description: A terminal / code window frame (traffic-light title bar) around its body. Great for commands, logs and code on dev slides.
module.aiSpec:
Wrap content in a TERMINAL / code-window frame with a macOS-style title bar. Block module: the body is the window contents — typically a fenced code block (```), a command, or log output; it renders as normal Markdown. Params: `title` — the title-bar text (e.g. bash, deploy.sh; default empty). Use it to make code/commands look like a real terminal. For plain code without the window chrome, just use a fenced code block instead.
<!-- @terminal title: bash --> ``` $ npm run build ✓ built in 1.2s ``` <!-- @end -->

param.name: title
param.label: Title bar text
param.desc: Text shown in the window title bar, e.g. bash or deploy.sh (empty = no title).

snippet.label: Terminal window
snippet.text: <!-- @terminal title: bash --> ``` $ npm install $ npm run dev ``` <!-- @end -->
snippet.description: A terminal window frame around commands / code / logs.
